prompt: Write an R&B song about wanting someone to stay through the night instead of leaving while things are unresolved.
response:
Stay Until the Morning

[Intro]
(Soft falsetto layers)

I don't wanna fight the silence
When your body's next to mine
Every breath is saying something
But your lips won't cross the line

[Verse 1]
You trace circles on my shoulder
Like you're scared to let me in
Every touch is getting colder
Where do we begin again?

We been talking in our sleep
Dancing 'round the truth we keep
If love's still alive in this room
Why's it feeling incomplete?

[Pre-Chorus]
Tell me if your heart's still here
Or if it's halfway out the door
I can feel you disappearing
Like footprints on the shore

If you're leaving, say it now
Don't let the night decide
I'd rather break in honesty
Than slowly drift apart inside

[Chorus]
Stay until the morning
Don't let this fade to black
If we're falling out of love
Baby, we can fight our way back

Stay until the sunrise
Let the light expose the truth
If there's still a spark between us
We don't have to let it loose

Don't walk away in the dark
While there's still fire in my heart

Stay... until the morning

[Verse 2]
Your perfume still on my t-shirt
Like a memory I can't fold
We used to burn like wildfire
Now we're barely feeling warm

Every promise in the kitchen
Every tear on bathroom floors
I don't wanna be a chapter
That you don't revisit anymore

[Pre-Chorus 2]
Tell me what you're scared of
Is it me or losing control?
'Cause I'd rather face the thunder
Than be left in the unknown

If you're breaking, let me hold you
Don't shut me outside
We can mend what's cracked in pieces
If we're brave enough to try

[Chorus]
Stay until the morning
Don't let this fade to black
If we're falling out of love
Baby, we can fight our way back

Stay until the sunrise
Let the light expose the truth
If there's still a spark between us
We don't have to let it loose

Don't walk away in the dark
While there's still fire in my heart

Stay... until the morning

[Bridge]
If love is just a season
Tell me why it hurts like this
Why your hands still fit in mine
Like they always did

Maybe we're just tired
Maybe we're afraid
But I still see forever
Every time you say my name

[Final Chorus]
Stay until the morning
Don't let this fade to black
Even if we're barely holding
We can pull each other back

Stay until the sunrise
Let the light show what we are
If there's even one more heartbeat
We don't have to fall apart

[Outro]
Don't you leave me in the dark...
Don't let love fall apart...
Stay... stay... stay...

metadata.title: Stay Until the Morning
metadata.subgenre: moody R&B with subtle gospel undertones

prompt: Describe the production arrangement for a moody late-night R&B track at 72 BPM in F# minor.
response:
Production spec for 'Stay Until the Morning':

- BPM: 72
- Key: F# minor
- Feel: Intimate late-night vulnerable

Instruments:
- Rhodes piano with wide stereo tremolo (main harmonic bed)
- Subby legato bass (warm, round, minimal movement)
- Analog pad (atmospheric, slow attack, fills the stereo field)
- Soft rim snare (not a full crack, more felt than heard)

Harmony:
- 9th chords and maj7 extensions throughout
- Avoid basic triads — keep everything suspended and unresolved
- Gospel-influenced voice leading in pre-chorus

Production philosophy:
- Sparse warm production — resist filling space
- Silence between phrases IS the emotion
- Let the Rhodes and vocal carry verses alone
- Build density gradually into choruses
- Bridge: strip back to just Rhodes + vocal before final chorus
